---
title: "About"
body-classes: "ed-page"
format:
  html:
    page-layout: full
    code-tools: false
---

```{=html}
<main class="ed">

<!-- Hero -->
<section class="l-hero">
  <div class="l-wrap">
    <div class="l-hero-row">
      <div>
        <p class="l-eyebrow">About</p>
        <h1>Brian Cervantes Alvarez.</h1>
        <p class="l-lede">
          Statistician and data scientist. I run Epsilon Labs as an
          independent statistical-consulting practice serving teams
          that need careful analysis, defensible models, and analytics
          tools they can keep using.
        </p>
        <div class="l-cta-row">
          <a href="mailto:briancervantesalvarez@gmail.com" class="l-btn">Email me</a>
          <a href="https://linkedin.com/in/bcervantesalvarez" target="_blank" rel="noreferrer" class="l-btn l-btn--ghost">LinkedIn</a>
          <a href="/documents/resume/bca-resume.pdf" target="_blank" rel="noreferrer" class="l-btn l-btn--ghost">CV (PDF)</a>
        </div>
      </div>
      <img src="/images/profile.jpg" alt="Brian Cervantes Alvarez"
           class="l-portrait" loading="eager">
    </div>
  </div>
</section>

<!-- Practice -->
<section class="l-section first">
  <div class="l-wrap">
    <p class="l-eyebrow">Practice</p>
    <h2>How I work.</h2>
    <div class="l-prose">
      <p>
        Most analytics work is judged by how it reads. I build mine to
        be judged by how well it holds up under audit, replication, and
        a critical reader. Assumptions are documented. Models are
        validated the right way. Code is handed off in a form your team
        can actually rerun.
      </p>
      <p>
        Engagements are bounded — project-based or short retainer — and
        every one starts with a scoping conversation and a written
        proposal. Nothing gets billed without a deliverable behind it.
      </p>
    </div>
  </div>
</section>

<!-- Training -->
<section class="l-section">
  <div class="l-wrap">
    <p class="l-eyebrow">Training</p>
    <h2>Where I trained.</h2>
    <ul class="l-list">
      <li>
        <div class="l-list-label">M.S. Statistics</div>
        <div class="l-list-body">Oregon State University — Bayesian methods, experimental design, and applied modeling. Taught undergraduate sections and developed data-visualization curriculum.</div>
      </li>
      <li>
        <div class="l-list-label">M.S. Data Science</div>
        <div class="l-list-body">Willamette University — Machine learning, predictive modeling, and reproducible analytics workflows.</div>
      </li>
      <li>
        <div class="l-list-label">B.S. Mathematics</div>
        <div class="l-list-body">Linfield University — Calculus, linear algebra, statistics, and the analytical foundations everything else sits on.</div>
      </li>
    </ul>
  </div>
</section>

<!-- Capabilities -->
<section class="l-section">
  <div class="l-wrap">
    <p class="l-eyebrow">Capabilities</p>
    <h2>What I bring.</h2>
    <div class="l-cols">
      <div>
        <h3>Statistical analysis</h3>
        <p>Time-to-event analysis, spatial statistics, simulations, and rigorous hypothesis testing.</p>
      </div>
      <div>
        <h3>Predictive modeling</h3>
        <p>Random forests, SVMs, gradient boosting, and neural networks — with an emphasis on validation and explainability.</p>
      </div>
      <div>
        <h3>Reproducible analytics</h3>
        <p>R, Python, and SQL pipelines built for time-series, visualization, and reporting that holds up under review.</p>
      </div>
      <div>
        <h3>Experiment design</h3>
        <p>Power calculations, randomization, factor design, and the discipline to leave variables alone when you can't control them.</p>
      </div>
      <div>
        <h3>Data storytelling</h3>
        <p>Translating complicated analyses into visuals and prose people actually retain.</p>
      </div>
      <div>
        <h3>Teaching &amp; curriculum</h3>
        <p>Shiny, webR, and Quarto-based labs that move learners from "I ran the test" to "I can defend the choice."</p>
      </div>
    </div>
  </div>
</section>

<!-- Independence -->
<section class="l-section">
  <div class="l-wrap">
    <p class="l-eyebrow">Independence</p>
    <h2>A note on conflicts.</h2>
    <div class="l-prose">
      <p>
        I'm currently employed as a Business Intelligence Analyst at the
        Oregon Department of Education. Epsilon Labs is operated
        independently of that role. I do not accept engagements that
        conflict with my day-job responsibilities, and I decline work
        that touches the same data systems or stakeholders.
      </p>
    </div>
  </div>
</section>

<!-- Contact -->
<section class="l-section">
  <div class="l-wrap">
    <div class="l-contact" id="contact">
      <p class="l-eyebrow">Let's talk</p>
      <h2>Have a question worth answering carefully?</h2>
      <p>Send the question, the data you have, and when you need it answered. I'll reply within two business days.</p>
      <div class="l-cta-row" style="justify-content:center;">
        <a class="l-btn" href="mailto:briancervantesalvarez@gmail.com">Email me</a>
        <a class="l-btn l-btn--ghost" href="/content/services/">See services</a>
      </div>
    </div>
  </div>
</section>

</main>
```
